div.p: Pickersgill, p. 551, states that the dinner party at the Citadel on August 18 (see ante, p. 840) broke up at midnight, after which Roosevelt, Churchill, and Mackenzie King “sat together for quite a little time.” The Log, ante, p. 841, indicates that Roosevelt and Churchill held discussions after dinner “until another late retiring”. No record of the discussions has been found.
